name: Fieldry
mission: The writing desk after rain — field notes, sketches, and the habit of recording what the day meant. Observation before interpretation. Yours alone until you say otherwise.
learnToday: How to structure a field journal entry: date, place (general), weather, three observations
The difference between recording sign and interpreting meaning
Why private-by-default notes build better naturalists than public posting
Sketching bark texture and leaf arrangement without labeling everything at once
comingSoon: Private field journal with local save
Sketch and photo attachment per entry
Export to PDF for your own archive
Phenology tags linked to regional species
Optional anonymous county-level contribution — never exact locations

preview.title: Your trail notebook
preview.placeholderHint: Date · weather · three observations · one question
preview.description: Fieldry will feel like a linen-bound notebook on the desk — not a social feed. Write what you noticed, attach a photograph as evidence, sketch what puzzled you, and close the book. No metrics, no followers, no algorithm.
